channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: More college applicants are opting to include SAT or ACT scores in their submissions
item.description: This application cycle, way more students reported test scores, up 10% compared with last year. That's despite many colleges having "test-optional" admissions policies.
item.content:encoded: This application cycle, way more students reported test scores, up 10% compared with last year. That's despite many colleges having "test-optional" admissions policies.
item.dc:creator: JD Allen

item.title: LACMA's sprawling new galleries encourage getting lost — and discovering new art
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang tours the new David Geffen Galleries at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art, with the museum's CEO and director Michael Govan.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang tours the new David Geffen Galleries at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art, with the museum's CEO and director Michael Govan.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Intagliata

item.title: A comedian launched a fake DHS tip line to report undocumented immigrants
item.description: Comedian Ben Palmer specializes in pranks. One of his more recent ideas for a prank was to set up a fake tip line for people to call in and report undocumented immigrants. He got hundreds of calls.
item.content:encoded: Comedian Ben Palmer specializes in pranks. One of his more recent ideas for a prank was to set up a fake tip line for people to call in and report undocumented immigrants. He got hundreds of calls.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: Meet the Tiny Desk finalists: Lauren Frihauf
item.description: Tiny Desk Contest judge Robin Hilton introduces us to finalist Lauren Frihauf from Denver, Colo.
item.content:encoded: Tiny Desk Contest judge Robin Hilton introduces us to finalist Lauren Frihauf from Denver, Colo.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Ceasefire at risk as U.S. helps ships run Iran blockade
item.description: The U.S. says it will guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz to break the deadlock.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. says it will guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz to break the deadlock.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Why U.S. troops are stationed in Germany, and what happens if some leave
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with Jeff Rathke, president of the American-German Institute, about U.S. troops stationed in Germany, and what happens if President Trump moves some of them elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Geiran

item.title: One man's quest to only forage for food for one year
item.description: A man who says he is only foraging for food for a year recently taught a group of people in Charlotte, N.C., about the plants that you can find and eat on a walk.
item.content:encoded: A man who says he is only foraging for food for a year recently taught a group of people in Charlotte, N.C., about the plants that you can find and eat on a walk.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Turner

item.title: The State Department has had a surge in new retirements
item.description: Usually, 2/3 of U.S. ambassadors come from the career foreign service. But the Trump administration has named mostly political ambassadors, leaving senior career diplomats with few prospects.
item.content:encoded: Usually, 2/3 of U.S. ambassadors come from the career foreign service. But the Trump administration has named mostly political ambassadors, leaving senior career diplomats with few prospects.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Saying goodbye to the Yankee broadcaster with the legendary calls
item.description: New York Yankees broadcaster John Sterling has died at age 87. He started as the team's play-by-play announcer in 1989, and he called every game until he missed his first one in 2019.
item.content:encoded: New York Yankees broadcaster John Sterling has died at age 87. He started as the team's play-by-play announcer in 1989, and he called every game until he missed his first one in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Megan Lim

item.title: In East Jerusalem, Israeli settlers use archaeological claims to evict Palestinians
item.description: Israeli settlers continue to push Palestinians from their homes and land where many have lived for generations. In East Jerusalem, the government uses archaeological claims to justify its orders.
item.content:encoded: Israeli settlers continue to push Palestinians from their homes and land where many have lived for generations. In East Jerusalem, the government uses archaeological claims to justify its orders.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley
